response: 263 is prime. It is not divisible by any prime numbers up to its square root (approximately 16.2), including 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, and 13.
reasoning_content: We need to check if 263 is prime. A quick sanity check: test divisibility by small primes. 263 is odd, so not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 2+6+3=11, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*37=259, remainder 4, so not divisible by 7. Check 11: 11*23=253, remainder 10, not divisible. Check 13: 13*20=260, remainder 3, not divisible. Check 17: 17*15=255, remainder 8. Check 19: 19*13=247, remainder 16. Check 23: 23*11=253, remainder 10. Check 29: 29*9=261, remainder 2. Check 31: 31*8=248, remainder 15. Next prime 37: 37*7=259, remainder 4. 41: 41*6=246, remainder 17. 43: 43*6=258, remainder 5. 47: 47*5=235, remainder 28. 53: 53*4=212, remainder 51. We only need to go up to sqrt(263) ≈ 16.2, so primes up to 13. Actually sqrt is about 16.2, so need to check primes up to 13? Wait 17 is >16.2? 17^2=289 >263, so we only need to check primes up to 13. Already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13. None divide. So 263 is prime. Answer: yes.
